allowed: Read and probe the current PR, Lite, SDK, providers, examples, lint, docs, and prior OKRA evidence
Write successor OKRA records in this isolated feature worktree
Edit Lite only after an accepted DKR proves a reusable primitive
Edit SDK core, providers, validation, tests, examples, docs, manifests, lockfile, and changesets required by the accepted design
Use deterministic fake adapters and local test fixtures
Run local gates and independent review
Commit and push narrow revisions to PR 346
approval_gates: Only the human may change the frame, metrics, thresholds, action envelope, release scope, or merge decision
A Lite core change requires an accepted DKR and independent challenge
Real external integration and publication remain human-gated
forbidden: Access or mutate real GitHub, production PostgreSQL, Victoria, or another external service
Add credentials, publish packages, cut releases, deploy, merge, or close the PR
Add MCP, automatic tool collection, implicit authority, hidden built-ins, shared scope factories, or module mocks
Add a Lite primitive without an accepted reusable proof
Change the objective, denominator, wall, threshold, or permission envelope silently
Force push, destructive reset, delete evidence, overwrite the dirty main worktree, or perform unrelated cleanup
action_envelope.rollback: Keep changes on feat/session-kernel in narrow commits; preserve main and predecessor evidence

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any logic, integration test, or public composition change
anti_goals.read_method: Reject module mocks, internal reaches, and global patches outside adapter unit tests
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Test imports, setup, and public API conformance checks

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any effectful flow, resource, tool, provider, queue, or publication change
anti_goals.read_method: Require every effectful child call to come from a declared controller, resource, or tag dependency
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Declared dependency graph, execution traces, and API-shape audit

anti_goals.activation_condition: Session activation close, cancel, release, checkpoint, or finish changes
anti_goals.read_method: Close without finish and assert durable status and version remain unchanged
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Session lifecycle conformance tests and ordered record traces

anti_goals.activation_condition: Concurrent session, turn, tool, event, continuation, or cancellation changes
anti_goals.read_method: Run two overlapping issue activations and compare runtime identities, tags, events, continuations, and terminal results

anti_goals.activation_condition: Repository, PostgreSQL, Victoria, GitHub, network, or process adapter changes
anti_goals.read_method: Require repository containment, read-only PostgreSQL, bounded Victoria reads, and verified idempotent GitHub publication
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Capability tests, fake backend call records, and authority-denial fixtures

anti_goals.activation_condition: Hypothesis, verification, or publication contract changes
anti_goals.read_method: Reject publication when any included hypothesis lacks fresh cited evidence or an independent verified verdict
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Evidence fixtures and deterministic publication-gate tests

anti_goals.activation_condition: Context, extension, trace, logging, or observation changes
anti_goals.read_method: Export only explicit safe observation projections and scan for config, credentials, authority details, prompts, and handles

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any DKR, design, readiness, progress, or completion acceptance
anti_goals.read_method: Reject claims backed only by the writer or orchestrator narrative
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Deterministic replay plus a separate validator or challenger record

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any Lite public API or lifecycle semantic change
anti_goals.coverage: reducing-DKR DKR-1 and DKR-2 and DKR-4
anti_goals.read_method: Count new primitives missing any required proof or carrying session-specific semantics
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Primitive evidence matrix, generic Lite tests, example use, and removed workaround diff

anti_goals.read_method: Run tests, typecheck, build, lint, and docs checks for every touched surface
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Focused package gates, root gates, and baseline comparison

anti_goals.activation_condition: Any source, test, example, or documentation change
anti_goals.read_method: Apply AGENTS.md naming, graph, scope, comments, type, docs, and README diagram rules
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Repository lint, touched-path audit, and independent review

anti_goals.read_method: Reject CKR promotion without accepted discovery and PKR promotion without its CKR and source checkpoint
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Run tree and accepted checkpoint references

anti_goals.activation_condition: Important evidence, worker output, run record, or product artifact is read or written
anti_goals.read_method: Use the store helper or record the target, source, and content hash; derive process counters from events
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Content hashes, helper records, worker progress, and store verification

anti_goals.activation_condition: Every move, metric read, checkpoint, and claim
anti_goals.read_method: Reject weakened, stale, unsupported, superficial, tautological, false-positive, or self-certified claims
anti_goals.source_of_truth: Append-only run store and independent deterministic evidence

human_confirmation.boundary: Human owns frame changes, real external access, release, merge, and retirement of standing walls.
human_confirmation.evidence: User accepted the frame and activated the goal after clarifying controller(flow), per-exec tags, and context observation.

anti_goals.source_of_truth: Named deterministic test, trace, audit, gate, or store record per wall

objective.lag_rule: Immediate after each accepted implementation slice and at terminalization
objective.read_method: Run the verifier and read its machine-readable passed and total fields
objective.source_of_truth: Saved issue-triage public-contract verifier at the current PR head

objective.definition: Count the named public contracts passed by the runnable GitHub issue-triage example at the current PR head.
